args.content:
import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Award(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='awards')
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
    
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 1", author=author1)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 2", author=author1)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 3", author=author1)
    
    Award.objects.create(name="Award 1", author=author1)
    Award.objects.create(name="Award 2", author=author1)

    # We expect 3 books and 2 awards.
    # Annotate count of books and max of awards.
    # Or just count of both.
    q = Author.objects.annotate(book_count=Count('books')).annotate(award_count=Count('awards'))
    res = q.first()
    print("Without distinct:")
    print(f"Books: {res.book_count} (Expected 3)")
    print(f"Awards: {res.award_count} (Expected 2)")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()

resultDisplay.newContent:
import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Award(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='awards')
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
    
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 1", author=author1)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 2", author=author1)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 3", author=author1)
    
    Award.objects.create(name="Award 1", author=author1)
    Award.objects.create(name="Award 2", author=author1)

    # We expect 3 books and 2 awards.
    # Annotate count of books and max of awards.
    # Or just count of both.
    q = Author.objects.annotate(book_count=Count('books')).annotate(award_count=Count('awards'))
    res = q.first()
    print("Without distinct:")
    print(f"Books: {res.book_count} (Expected 3)")
    print(f"Awards: {res.award_count} (Expected 2)")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()

import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
    
    from django.utils import timezone
    now = timezone.now()
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 1", author=author1, created_at=now)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 2", author=author1, created_at=now)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 3", author=author1, created_at=now)
    
    q = Author.objects.annotate(book_count=Count('books')).annotate(latest_book=Max('books__created_at'))
    print("SQL Query:", q.query)
    res = q.first()
    print("Books count:", res.book_count, "(Expected 3)")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()

import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Award(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='awards')
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
    
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 1", author=author1)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 2", author=author1)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 3", author=author1)
    
    Award.objects.create(name="Award 1", author=author1)
    Award.objects.create(name="Award 2", author=author1)

    # We expect 3 books and 2 awards.
    # Annotate count of books and max of awards.
    # Or just count of both.
    q = Author.objects.annotate(book_count=Count('books')).annotate(award_count=Count('awards'))
    res = q.first()
    print("Without distinct:")
    print(f"Books: {res.book_count} (Expected 3)")
    print(f"Awards: {res.award_count} (Expected 2)")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()
resultDisplay.newContent:
import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
    
    from django.utils import timezone
    now = timezone.now()
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 1", author=author1, created_at=now)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 2", author=author1, created_at=now)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 3", author=author1, created_at=now)
    
    q = Author.objects.annotate(book_count=Count('books')).annotate(latest_book=Max('books__created_at'))
    print("SQL Query:", q.query)
    res = q.first()
    print("Books count:", res.book_count, "(Expected 3)")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()

response.output:
Web search results for "site:code.djangoproject.com "inflated" "Count" "annotate" "reverse foreign keys"":

The search results point to a well-known behavior in the Django ORM where performing multiple `annotate()` calls with `Count` (or other aggregates like `Sum`) over **reverse foreign keys** (or many-to-many relationships) results in **inflated counts**.

### The Root Cause
When you annotate a queryset with multiple counts from different related tables, Django performs multiple `LEFT OUTER JOIN` operations.[1] Because these joins are not independent in the resulting SQL query, the rows from one join multiply the rows of the other. 

For example, if an object has 2 related "Books" and 3 related "Authors", a query annotating both counts will create a join that results in $2 \times 3 = 6$ rows for that object, causing both `Count` values to be reported as 6 instead of their actual values.

### Relevant Django Tickets & Discussions
*   **Ticket #10060:** This is the primary ticket discussing "Aggregate/Annotation results are wrong when multiple joins are involved."
*   **Ticket #19011:** Specifically mentions "multiple annotate Sum terms yields inflated answer."[2]
*   **Stack Overflow Consensus:** The community frequently refers to this as a "gotcha" where the ORM's join logic produces a Cartesian product.

### Recommended Solutions

1.  **Use `distinct=True`:**
    The simplest fix for `Count` is to add the `distinct` argument.[1] This ensures that only unique IDs from the related table are counted, effectively ignoring the duplicate rows created by the joins.
    ```python[3][2][4]
    # Instead of:
    Project.objects.annotate(num_tasks=Count('tasks'), num_members=Count('members'))
    
    # Use:
    Project.objects.annotate(
        num_tasks=Count('tasks', distinct=True),
        num_members=Count('members', distinct=True)
    )
    ```[4]

2.  **Use `Subquery` (Django 1.11+):**
    For more complex aggregations (like `Sum`) where `distinct=True` might not work or might be slow, use a `Subquery`. This keeps the aggregation logic isolated from the main query's joins.
    ```python
    from django.db.models import OuterRef, Subquery, Count[3]

    tasks_count = Task.objects.filter(project=OuterRef('pk')).values('project').annotate(c=Count('id')).values('c')
    Project.objects.annotate(num_tasks=Subquery(tasks_count))
    ```

3.  **Separate Queries:**
    If performance allows, fetching the counts in separate queries or using `prefetch_related` and counting in Python can avoid the SQL join complexity entirely.

Sources:
[1] stackoverflow.com (https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEFGM3x3WGMecYXOfp6cZxZZfFfMgoyoqiDGj6h-rKm9q99qHL6zdt_xfB3KQlYIDUiXeIFMj4nrE72-Dw44Q9919CWB43d-6GgMD-vjZ3PbAMKdy-rXz3jD8-ZvW6G6Nw_nh3Bcoc4x1qu4ZoG8O8zjLaPya7s5RYBFT9G6W64ADNOEHQdUKTSm8EMknHefDsp9rFd3HEuLbRuky-1J5wr2fxbgrU2F3Po1ptKbHFxqZDvZEJcIS39)

import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',

    USE_TZ=False,

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
    
    from django.utils import timezone
    now = timezone.now()
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 1", author=author1, created_at=now)

import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Award(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='awards')
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
    
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 1", author=author1)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 2", author=author1)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 3", author=author1)

    Award.objects.create(name="Award 1", author=author1)
    Award.objects.create(name="Award 2", author=author1)
    
    q = Author.objects.annotate(book_count=Count('books')).annotate(max_award=Max('awards__name'))
    print("SQL Query:", q.query)
    res = q.first()
    print("Books count:", res.book_count, "(Expected 3)")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()

import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
    
    from django.utils import timezone
    now = timezone.now()
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 1", author=author1, created_at=now)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 2", author=author1, created_at=now)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 3", author=author1, created_at=now)
    
    q = Author.objects.annotate(book_count=Count('books')).annotate(latest_book=Max('books__created_at'))
    print("SQL Query:", q.query)
    res = q.first()
    print("Books count:", res.book_count, "(Expected 3)")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()

args.content:
import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Tag(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Post(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    tags = models.ManyToManyField(Tag, related_name='posts')

    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    post = Post.objects.create(title="Post 1")
    
    tag1 = Tag.objects.create(name="Tag 1")
    tag2 = Tag.objects.create(name="Tag 2")
    tag3 = Tag.objects.create(name="Tag 3")
    
    post.tags.add(tag1, tag2, tag3)
    
    q = Post.objects.annotate(tag_count=Count('tags')).annotate(max_tag=Max('tags__created_at'))
    print("SQL Query:", q.query)
    res = q.first()

import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Award(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='awards')
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
    
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 1", author=author1)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 2", author=author1)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 3", author=author1)

    Award.objects.create(name="Award 1", author=author1)
    Award.objects.create(name="Award 2", author=author1)
    
    q = Author.objects.annotate(book_count=Count('books')).annotate(max_award=Max('awards__name'))
    print("SQL Query:", q.query)
    res = q.first()
    print("Books count:", res.book_count, "(Expected 3)")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()

import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Tag(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Post(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    tags = models.ManyToManyField(Tag, related_name='posts')

    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    post = Post.objects.create(title="Post 1")
    
    tag1 = Tag.objects.create(name="Tag 1")
    tag2 = Tag.objects.create(name="Tag 2")
    tag3 = Tag.objects.create(name="Tag 3")
    
    post.tags.add(tag1, tag2, tag3)
    
    q = Post.objects.annotate(tag_count=Count('tags')).annotate(max_tag=Max('tags__created_at'))
    print("SQL Query:", q.query)
    res = q.first()
    print("Tags count:", res.tag_count, "(Expected 3)")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()

response.output:
Web search results for ""explosion of rows before the aggregation occurs" django "inflated"":

In Django, the "explosion of rows" (often leading to **inflated** or **incorrect results**) occurs when you combine multiple `annotate()` calls that involve `ManyToManyField` or reverse `ForeignKey` relationships in a single QuerySet.

### The Cause: Cartesian Products
When you annotate a QuerySet with a related field, Django performs a `LEFT OUTER JOIN`. If you annotate with *two* different related fields (e.g., counting both "Comments" and "Likes" on a "Post"), the database creates a **Cartesian product** of the related rows.

For example, if a Post has **3** Likes and **2** Comments:
*   A single join for Likes would result in 3 rows.[1]
*   A single join for Comments would result in 2 rows.
*   **The "Explosion":** Joining both simultaneously results in **3 × 2 = 6 rows** before the aggregation occurs. Consequently, `Count('likes')` returns 6 and `Count('comments')` also returns 6, rather than 3 and 2.

---

### Solutions

#### 1. Use `distinct=True` (Best for `Count`)
The simplest fix for `Count` is to tell the database to only count unique primary keys.
```python
from django.db.models import Count[2]

# This prevents the inflated count
posts = Post.objects.annotate(
    likes_count=Count('likes', distinct=True),
    comments_count=Count('comments', distinct=True)
)
```

#### 2. Use `Subquery` (Best for `Sum`, `Avg`, or Performance)
`distinct=True` does not work for `Sum` or `Avg` (it would only sum the unique values, which is usually wrong). For these, or for better performance on large datasets, use subqueries to aggregate the data *before* joining it to the main query.
```python
from django.db.models import OuterRef, Subquery, Count

likes_subquery = Like.objects.filter(post=OuterRef('pk')).values('post').annotate(c=Count('id')).values('c')

posts = Post.objects.annotate(
    likes_count=Subquery(likes_subquery)
)
```

#### 3. Use `FilteredRelation` (Django 2.0+)
If you are aggregating the same relationship with different filters, `FilteredRelation` can sometimes help keep the joins cleaner, though it is more specialized.

### Summary
*   **The Symptom:** Your counts or sums are much higher than expected (often multiples of each other).
*   **The Reason:** Multiple `LEFT JOINs` creating a row for every possible combination of related items.
*   **The Quick Fix:** Add `distinct=True` to your `Count()` calls.[2][1]

Sources:
[1] stackoverflow.com (https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQGmpkh5_0GlwpAUvfEVbGyGm1fKJ03qtyDIM4on3DEn_BLT8s3k9OOgBLLbhKxe5u-QmKpi3ZmNewYp9ZvWZMNYN49HmbdmD3WUWXG1ithupgPuT0cqbrZ7cKIjTObruP5blkOuWOBJ_tYZDDQP8m5h9jZggnVzQ4n0Gz5o2xFmATlILPO5581EPrSrXDfS5wE6gdUXPTz0VBPAvfQ=)
[2] codewithmosh.com (https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQH6tfsgq_eU341-BPUVf1-kP3FEaQ8QLBXur2omtIDG4dJPzsUJqMxxzbir0SuIar6nEzEW_4teQLYFAm3kFYY4uSDyFZYV9yUfupBKxjarOYDT-NWSCNbfQbch5FbJ357M_wb1AZ0JLGg05u5ZrtizZcjj4E6_JTkrjrRC5-FCzbJ6GPKvSqE5pNhqL4kiA6HN7yJt)

import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
    
    from django.utils import timezone
    now = timezone.now()
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 1", author=author1, created_at=now)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 2", author=author1, created_at=now)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 3", author=author1, created_at=now)
    
    q = Author.objects.annotate(book_count=Count('books', distinct=True)).annotate(latest_book=Max('books__created_at'))
    print("SQL Query:", q.query)
    res = q.first()
    print("Books count:", res.book_count, "(Expected 3)")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()

    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Tag(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Post(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    tags = models.ManyToManyField(Tag, related_name='posts')

    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    post = Post.objects.create(title="Post 1")
    
    tag1 = Tag.objects.create(name="Tag 1")
    tag2 = Tag.objects.create(name="Tag 2")
    tag3 = Tag.objects.create(name="Tag 3")
    
    post.tags.add(tag1, tag2, tag3)
    
    q = Post.objects.annotate(tag_count=Count('tags')).annotate(max_tag=Max('tags__created_at'))
    print("SQL Query:", q.query)
    res = q.first()
    print("Tags count:", res.tag_count, "(Expected 3)")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()
resultDisplay.newContent:
import os
import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'reproduce_app',
    ],
    USE_TZ=False,
)

django.setup()

from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)

from django.db.models import Count, Max

def run():
    author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
    
    from django.utils import timezone
    now = timezone.now()
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 1", author=author1, created_at=now)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 2", author=author1, created_at=now)
    Book.objects.create(title="Book 3", author=author1, created_at=now)
    
    q = Author.objects.annotate(book_count=Count('books', distinct=True)).annotate(latest_book=Max('books__created_at'))
    print("SQL Query:", q.query)
    res = q.first()
    print("Books count:", res.book_count, "(Expected 3)")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()

args.query: Django "QuerySet.annotate() with Count returns inaccurate inflated integers when mixed with other aggregations on reverse foreign keys"

response.output:
Web search results for "Django "QuerySet.annotate() with Count returns inaccurate inflated integers when mixed with other aggregations on reverse foreign keys"":

I will start by listing the files in the current directory to understand the project structure.
I will start by checking the current directory to see if there's a Django project.
I will check the current directory to see if there's a specific project I should be working on.
This is a well-documented behavior in Django's ORM.[1] When you use `annotate()` with multiple `Count` aggregations on reverse foreign keys, Django performs multiple `LEFT OUTER JOIN` operations. This creates a **Cartesian product** in the underlying SQL, causing the counts to be multiplied by each other (inflated).

### Why it happens
If a `Parent` has 2 `ChildA` records and 3 `ChildB` records, a single query joining both will result in $2 \times 3 = 6$ rows.[1] A standard `Count()` will then return `6` for both instead of `2` and `3`.[1]

### Solutions

#### 1. Use `distinct=True` (Simplest)
Adding `distinct=True` to the `Count` call tells the database to only count unique primary keys from the related table, effectively "collapsing" the Cartesian product for that specific count.[1]

```python
from django.db.models import Count[1][2][3][4][5][6][7][8]

# Inflated results
qs = Parent.objects.annotate(
    count_a=Count('childa'),
    count_b=Count('childb')
)

# Correct results
qs = Parent.objects.annotate(
    count_a=Count('childa', distinct=True),
    count_b=Count('childb', distinct=True)
)
```

#### 2. Use `Subquery` (More Robust/Performant)
For larger datasets or more complex aggregations (like `Sum`), `distinct=True` might not be enough or may be slow. Using a `Subquery` performs the count in a separate SQL context, avoiding the join issue entirely.[1]

```python
from django.db.models import OuterRef, Subquery, Count[9][1]

child_a_count = ChildA.objects.filter(
    parent=OuterRef('pk')
).order_by().values('parent').annotate(
    c=Count('id')
).values('c')

qs = Parent.objects.annotate(
    count_a=Subquery(child_a_count)
)
```

**Would you like me to help you apply one of these fixes to a specific part of your codebase, or would you like a full reproduction script to verify this behavior?**

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models
class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'
class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
Book.objects.create(author=author1)
Book.objects.create(author=author1)

q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('book')).annotate(m=Max('book__created_at'))
print("Len of list:", len(list(q)))
print("qs.count():", q.count())

args.content:
import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Tag(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    tags = models.ManyToManyField(Tag, related_name='books')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")

tag1 = Tag.objects.create(name="Tag 1")
tag2 = Tag.objects.create(name="Tag 2")

b1 = Book.objects.create(author=author1)
b2 = Book.objects.create(author=author1)
b3 = Book.objects.create(author=author1)

b1.tags.add(tag1, tag2)
b2.tags.add(tag1)

# So b1 has 2 tags, b2 has 1 tag, b3 has 0 tags. Total joins for author1 will be 2 (for b1) + 1 (for b2) + 1 (for b3, outer join) = 4 rows?
# Or 2 + 1 + 1 = 4 rows.
q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('books')).annotate(m=Max('books__tags__created_at'))
print(q.query)
print("Books count:", q.first().c, "(Expected 3)")

# So b1 has 2 tags, b2 has 1 tag, b3 has 0 tags. Total joins for author1 will be 2 (for b1) + 1 (for b2) + 1 (for b3, outer join) = 4 rows?
# Or 2 + 1 + 1 = 4 rows.
q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('books')).annotate(m=Max('books__tags__created_at'))
print(q.query)
print("Books count:", q.first().c, "(Expected 3)")

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models
class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'
class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
Book.objects.create(author=author1)
Book.objects.create(author=author1)

q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('book__id')).annotate(m=Max('book__created_at'))
print(q.query)
print(q.first().c)

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Chapter(models.Model):
    book = models.ForeignKey(Book, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='chapters')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")

b1 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 1")
b2 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 2")
b3 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 3")

Chapter.objects.create(book=b1)
Chapter.objects.create(book=b1)
Chapter.objects.create(book=b2)
Chapter.objects.create(book=b3)

q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('books')).annotate(m=Max('books__chapters__created_at'))
print(q.query)
print("Books count:", q.first().c, "(Expected 3)")

args.content:
import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models
class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'
class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")

from django.utils import timezone
from datetime import timedelta
now = timezone.now()

Book.objects.create(author=author1, created_at=now)
Book.objects.create(author=author1, created_at=now + timedelta(days=1))
Book.objects.create(author=author1, created_at=now + timedelta(days=2))

q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('books')).order_by('books__created_at')
print(q.query)
print("Books count for first row:", q.first().c, "(Expected 3)")

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models
class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'
class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")

from django.utils import timezone
from datetime import timedelta
now = timezone.now()

Book.objects.create(author=author1, created_at=now)
Book.objects.create(author=author1, created_at=now + timedelta(days=1))
Book.objects.create(author=author1, created_at=now + timedelta(days=2))

q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('books')).order_by('books__created_at')
print(q.query)
print("Books count for first row:", q.first().c, "(Expected 3)")

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models
class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'
class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    pages = models.IntegerField(default=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Sum

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")

Book.objects.create(author=author1, pages=100)
Book.objects.create(author=author1, pages=200)

q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('books')).annotate(s=Sum('books__pages'))
print(q.query)
print("Books count:", q.first().c, "(Expected 2)")

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models
class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'
class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    pages = models.IntegerField(default=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Sum

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")

Book.objects.create(author=author1, pages=100)
Book.objects.create(author=author1, pages=200)

q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('books')).annotate(s=Sum('books__pages'))
print(q.query)
print("Books count:", q.first().c, "(Expected 2)")

toolCalls.name: codebase_investigator

args.objective: Investigate the bug where QuerySet.annotate() with Count returns inaccurate inflated integers when mixed with other aggregations on reverse foreign keys. The bug mentions this is a deep architectural issue with how Django constructs the GROUP BY clause in django.db.models.sql.query.Query and compiler.py. Find the exact issue and propose a fix.

toolCalls.resultDisplay:
- ** Summary Of Findings**: The investigation was interrupted, but the core issue was identified as a classic multi-table aggregation problem in Django's ORM. When multiple aggregations (like `Count`) are performed on different reverse foreign keys or many-to-many relations, Django generates multiple `LEFT OUTER JOIN`s. This results in a Cartesian product of the related rows in the intermediate result set. For example, if an Author has 3 Books and 2 Awards, joining both tables produces 6 rows for that Author. A `Count('books')` will then return 6 instead of 3.
  
  The root cause is located in `django.db.models.sql.query.Query`, where `add_annotation` resolves expressions and adds joins to the global `alias_map`. The architectural flaw is that Django does not isolate independent one-to-many joins used for aggregation.
  
  Proposed Fix:
  The recommended fix is to move away from using joins in the main query for these types of aggregations and instead use subqueries. Internally, when `Query.add_annotation` detects an aggregate traversal of a 'to-many' relation, it should ideally wrap the aggregation in a subquery expression (similar to how a developer might manually use `Subquery` and `OuterRef`). This would isolate the join and the count logic, preventing the Cartesian product from affecting other annotations. Short-term, improving documentation or adding a warning when multiple multi-valued joins are detected for annotations could help, but a robust architectural fix requires subquery-based aggregation by default for multi-valued relations.
- ** Exploration Trace**:
  - Examined `reproduce.py` and `test_count.py` to understand the reported bug. The tests show that `Count` returns inflated values when combined with other reverse relationship lookups.
  - Analyzed `django/db/models/sql/query.py`, specifically `add_annotation` and `setup_joins`. These methods are responsible for adding annotations to the query and creating the necessary SQL joins.
  - Reviewed `django/db/models/aggregates.py` to see how `Count` and other aggregates are implemented. `Count` uses a simple `COUNT(alias.id)` which is susceptible to inflation in Cartesian products.
  - Identified the core issue as the ORM's reliance on `LEFT OUTER JOIN` for reverse relationship aggregations. When multiple such relationships are joined, a Cartesian product is created before aggregation, leading to inflated counts.
- ** Relevant Locations**:
  |  File Path |  Reasoning |  Key Symbols |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | django/db/models/sql/query.py | This file contains the logic for resolving field paths into joins and adding annotations. The current implementation adds joins directly to the main query, which causes the Cartesian product issue. | ["add_annotation","setup_joins","names_to_path"] |
  | django/db/models/sql/compiler.py | The compiler is responsible for generating the final SQL, including the GROUP BY clause. The architectural issue involves how the compiler treats columns from multiple independent one-to-many joins. | ["get_group_by","execute_sql"] |
  | django/db/models/aggregates.py | The aggregate classes define how the SQL for the aggregation is generated. Changing how they interact with the query or forcing them to use subqueries could be part of the solution. | ["Count","Aggregate.as_sql"] |

args.content:
import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")

Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 1")
Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 2")
Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 3")

q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('books')).values('name', 'c').annotate(m=Max('books__created_at'))
print(q.query)
for row in q:
    print(row)

django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models
class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'
class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    pages = models.IntegerField(default=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")

Book.objects.create(author=author1, pages=100)
Book.objects.create(author=author1, pages=200)

q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('books')).filter(books__pages__gt=10).annotate(m=Max('books__pages'))
print(q.query)
print("Books count:", q.first().c, "(Expected 2)")

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Chapter(models.Model):
    book = models.ForeignKey(Book, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='chapters')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
b1 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 1")
b2 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 2")
b3 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 3")
Chapter.objects.create(book=b1)
Chapter.objects.create(book=b1)

q = Author.objects.values('books__title').annotate(c=Count('books')).annotate(m=Max('books__chapters__created_at'))
print(q.query)
for row in q:
    print(row)

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Chapter(models.Model):
    book = models.ForeignKey(Book, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='chapters')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
b1 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 1")
b2 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 2")
b3 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 3")
Chapter.objects.create(book=b1)
Chapter.objects.create(book=b1)

q = Author.objects.values('books__title').annotate(c=Count('books')).annotate(m=Max('books__chapters__created_at'))
print(q.query)
for row in q:
    print(row)

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Chapter(models.Model):
    book = models.ForeignKey(Book, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='chapters')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
b1 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 1")
b2 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 2")
b3 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 3")
Chapter.objects.create(book=b1)
Chapter.objects.create(book=b1)

q = Author.objects.values('books__title').annotate(c=Count('books', distinct=True)).annotate(m=Max('books__chapters__created_at'))
print(q.query)
for row in q:
    print(row)

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Chapter(models.Model):
    book = models.ForeignKey(Book, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='chapters')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")

b1 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 1")
b2 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 2")
b3 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 3")

Chapter.objects.create(book=b1)
Chapter.objects.create(book=b1)
Chapter.objects.create(book=b2)
Chapter.objects.create(book=b3)

q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('books', distinct=True)).annotate(m=Max('books__chapters__created_at'))
q.query.get_compiler('default').connection.features.allows_group_by_pk = False
print(q.query)
print("Books count:", q.first().c, "(Expected 3)")

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Chapter(models.Model):
    book = models.ForeignKey(Book, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='chapters')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

author1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
b1 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 1")
b2 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 2")
b3 = Book.objects.create(author=author1, title="Book 3")
Chapter.objects.create(book=b1)
Chapter.objects.create(book=b1)

q = Book.objects.values('author').annotate(c=Count('id')).annotate(m=Max('chapters__created_at'))
print(q.query)
for row in q:
    print(row)

class Author(models.Model):

class Book(models.Model):

    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Chapter(models.Model):
    book = models.ForeignKey(Book, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='chapters')

args.content:
import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models
class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'
class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    pages = models.IntegerField(default=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Sum

a1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
a2 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 2")

Book.objects.create(author=a1, pages=100)
Book.objects.create(author=a1, pages=200)

Book.objects.create(author=a2, pages=50)

q = Author.objects.values('name').annotate(c=Count('books')).annotate(s=Sum('books__pages'))
print(q.query)
for row in q:
    print(row)

import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))

import django
from django.conf import settings

django.setup()
from django.db import models
class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'
class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    pages = models.IntegerField(default=100)

    print(row)

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

a1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
b1 = Book.objects.create(author=a1, title="Book 1")
b2 = Book.objects.create(author=a1, title="Book 2")
b3 = Book.objects.create(author=a1, title="Book 3")

q = Book.objects.filter(id=b1.id).annotate(c_author=Count('author')).annotate(c_books=Count('author__books'))
print(q.query)
res = q.first()
print("Author count for Book 1:", res.c_author, "(Expected 1)")
print("Books count for Author:", res.c_books, "(Expected 3)")

toolCalls.name: run_shell_command

args.description: Reading get_group_by to spot the bug.

response.output:
Output:         # a chosen GROUP BY clause - you can almost do this by using the form:
        #     .values(*wanted_cols).annotate(AnAggregate())
        # but any later annotations, extra selects, values calls that
        # refer some column outside of the wanted_cols, order_by, or even
        # filter calls can alter the GROUP BY clause.

        # The query.group_by is either None (no GROUP BY at all), True
        # (group by select fields), or a list of expressions to be added
        # to the group by.
        if self.query.group_by is None:
            return []
        expressions = []
        if self.query.group_by is not True:
            # If the group by is set to a list (by .values() call most likely),
            # then we need to add everything in it to the GROUP BY clause.
            # Backwards compatibility hack for setting query.group_by. Remove
            # when  we have public API way of forcing the GROUP BY clause.
            # Converts string references to expressions.
            for expr in self.query.group_by:
                if not hasattr(expr, 'as_sql'):
                    expressions.append(self.query.resolve_ref(expr))
                else:
                    expressions.append(expr)
        # Note that even if the group_by is set, it is only the minimal
        # set to group by. So, we need to add cols in select, order_by, and
        # having into the select in any case.
        for expr, _, _ in select:
            cols = expr.get_group_by_cols()
            for col in cols:
                expressions.append(col)
        for expr, (sql, params, is_ref) in order_by:
            # Skip References to the select clause, as all expressions in the
            # select clause are already part of the group by.
            if not expr.contains_aggregate and not is_ref:
                expressions.extend(expr.get_source_expressions())
        having_group_by = self.having.get_group_by_cols() if self.having else ()
        for expr in having_group_by:
            expressions.append(expr)
        result = []
        seen = set()
        expressions = self.collapse_group_by(expressions, having_group_by)

        for expr in expressions:
            sql, params = self.compile(expr)
            if isinstance(expr, Subquery) and not sql.startswith('('):

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Award(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='awards')
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

a1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
Book.objects.create(author=a1, title="Book 1")
Book.objects.create(author=a1, title="Book 2")
Award.objects.create(author=a1, name="Award 1")
Award.objects.create(author=a1, name="Award 2")

q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('books')).annotate(m=Max('awards__name'))
print(q.query)
print("Books count:", q.first().c, "(Expected 2)")

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Award(models.Model):
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='awards')
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath('django_repo'))
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
from django.conf import settings

settings.configure(DEBUG=True, DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}, INSTALLED_APPS=['reproduce_app'], USE_TZ=False)
django.setup()
from django.db import models

class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

class Book(models.Model):
    author1 = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books1')
    author2 = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books2', null=True)
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    class Meta: app_label = 'reproduce_app'

from django.core.management import call_command
call_command('makemigrations', 'reproduce_app', interactive=False)
call_command('migrate', interactive=False)
from django.db.models import Count, Max

a1 = Author.objects.create(name="Author 1")
Book.objects.create(author1=a1, author2=a1, title="Book 1")
Book.objects.create(author1=a1, author2=a1, title="Book 2")

q = Author.objects.annotate(c=Count('books1')).annotate(m=Max('books2__created_at'))
print(q.query)
print("Books count:", q.first().c, "(Expected 2)")
